<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # GitLab
2: 
3: ## Canonical source
4: 
5: The canonical source of GitLab where all development takes place is [hosted on GitLab.com](https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab).
6: 
7: If you wish to clone a copy of GitLab without proprietary code, you can use the read-only mirror of GitLab located at https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-foss/. However, please do not submit any issues and/or merge requests to that project.
8: 
9: ## Free trial
10: 
11: You can request a free trial of GitLab Ultimate [on our website](https://about.gitlab.com/free-trial/).
12: 
13: ## Open source software to collaborate on code
14: 
15: To see how GitLab looks please see the [features page on our website](https://about.gitlab.com/features/).
16: 
17: - Manage Git repositories with fine grained access controls that keep your code secure
18: - Perform code reviews and enhance collaboration with merge requests
19: - Complete continuous integration (CI) and continuous deployment/delivery (CD) pipelines to build, test, and deploy your applications
20: - Each project can also have an issue tracker, issue board, and a wiki
21: - Used by more than 100,000 organizations, GitLab is the most popular solution to manage Git repositories on-premises
22: - Open-core, most of the code is MIT with the files in `/ee` being proprietary but source available and open for contributions.
23: 
24: ## Editions
25: 
26: There are three editions of GitLab:
27: 
28: - GitLab Community Edition (CE) is available freely under the MIT Expat license.
29: - GitLab Enterprise Edition (EE) includes [extra features](https://about.gitlab.com/pricing/#compare-options) that are more useful for organizations with more than 100 users. To use EE and get official support please [become a subscriber](https://about.gitlab.com/pricing/).
30: - JiHu Edition (JH) tailored specifically for the [Chinese market](https://about.gitlab.cn/).
31: 
32: ## Licensing
33: 
34: See the [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for licensing information as it pertains to
35: files in this repository.
36: 
37: ## Hiring
38: 
39: We are hiring developers, support engineers, and production engineers all the time, please see our [jobs page](https://about.gitlab.com/jobs/).
40: 
41: ## Website
42: 
43: On [about.gitlab.com](https://about.gitlab.com/) you can find more information about:
44: 
45: - [Subscriptions](https://about.gitlab.com/pricing/)
46: - [Professional Services](https://about.gitlab.com/services/)
47: - [Community](https://about.gitlab.com/community/)
48: - [Hosted GitLab.com](https://about.gitlab.com/gitlab-com/) use GitLab as a free service
49: - [GitLab Enterprise Edition](https://about.gitlab.com/features/#enterprise) with additional features aimed at larger organizations.
50: - [GitLab CI](https://about.gitlab.com/solutions/continuous-integration/) a continuous integration (CI) server that is easy to integrate with GitLab.
51: 
52: ## Requirements
53: 
54: Please see the [requirements documentation](doc/install/requirements.md) for system requirements and more information about the supported operating systems.
55: 
56: ## Installation
57: 
58: The recommended way to install GitLab is with the [Omnibus packages](https://docs.gitlab.com/install/package/) on our package server.
59: Compared to [a self-compiled installation](https://docs.gitlab.com/install/self_compiled/), this is faster and less error prone.
60: Just select your operating system, download the respective package (Debian or RPM) and install it using the system's package manager.
61: 
62: For more information about other installation methods, see [install GitLab](https://about.gitlab.com/install/).
63: 
64: ## Contributing
65: 
66: GitLab is an open source project and we are very happy to accept community contributions. Please refer to the [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](CONTRIBUTING.md) file for more details.
67: 
68: ## Install a development environment
69: 
70: To work on GitLab itself, we recommend setting up your development environment with [the GitLab Development Kit (GDK)](https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-development-kit).
71: If you do not use GDK, you need to [install and configure all the dependencies yourself](https://docs.gitlab.com/install/installation/), this is a lot of work and error prone.
72: One small thing you also have to do when installing it yourself is to copy the example development Puma configuration file:
73: 
74: ```shell
75: cp config/puma.example.development.rb config/puma.rb
76: ```
77: 
78: Instructions on how to start GitLab and how to run the tests can be found in the [getting started section of the GitLab Development Kit](https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-development-kit#getting-started).
79: 
80: ## Software stack
81: 
82: GitLab is a Ruby on Rails application that runs on the following software:
83: 
84: - Ubuntu/Debian/CentOS/RHEL/OpenSUSE
85: - Ruby (MRI) 3.3.10
86: - Git 2.33+
87: - Redis 6.0+
88: - PostgreSQL 16.5+
89: 
90: For more information please see the [architecture](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/development/architecture.html) and [requirements](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/install/requirements.html) documentation.
91: 
92: ## UX design
93: 
94: Please adhere to the [UX Guide](https://design.gitlab.com/) when creating designs and implementing code.
95: 
96: ## Third-party applications
97: 
98: There are a lot of [third-party applications integrating with GitLab](https://about.gitlab.com/applications/). These include GUI Git clients, mobile applications and API wrappers for various languages.
99: 
100: ## GitLab release cycle
101: 
102: For more information about the release process see the [release documentation](https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/release-tools/blob/master/README.md).
103: 
104: ## Upgrading
105: 
106: For upgrading information please see our [update page](https://about.gitlab.com/update/).
107: 
108: ## Documentation
109: 
110: All documentation can be found on <https://docs.gitlab.com>.
111: 
112: ## Education
113: 
114: Get up to speed quickly with comprehensive learning resources at [GitLab University](https://university.gitlab.com).
115: 
116: ## Getting help
117: 
118: Please see [Getting help for GitLab](https://about.gitlab.com/getting-help/) on our website for the many options to get help.
119: 
120: ## Why should I use GitLab?
121: 
122: Read [why our customers choose GitLab](https://about.gitlab.com/why-gitlab/).

(End of file - total 122 lines)
</content>

320:       [:unchecked, :cannot_be_merged_recheck, :checking, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking].include?(merge_status.to_sym)
321:     end
322: 
323:     # rubocop: disable Style/SymbolProc
324:     before_transition { |merge_request| merge_request.enable_transitioning }
325: 
326:     after_transition { |merge_request| merge_request.disable_transitioning }
327:     # rubocop: enable Style/SymbolProc
328:   end
329: 
330:   # NOTE: Batch transition merge_status to unchecked/cannot_be_merged_recheck.
331:   # This bypasses state machine callbacks for performance but mimics their behavior.
332:   #
333:   # Transitions mirror the `mark_as_unchecked` event defined above.
334:   # Process batch updates in batches as there can be many target merge requests.
335:   def self.batch_mark_as_unchecked(mr_ids, mrs_to_trigger: [], batch_size: 1000)
336:     return if mr_ids.blank?
337: 
338:     mr_ids.each_slice(batch_size) do |batch_ids|
339:       where(id: batch_ids)
340:         .where(merge_status: [:preparing, :can_be_merged, :checking])
341:         .update_all(merge_status: :unchecked)
342: 
343:       where(id: batch_ids)
344:         .where(merge_status: [:cannot_be_merged, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking])
345:         .update_all(merge_status: :cannot_be_merged_recheck)
346:     end
347: 
348:     mrs_to_trigger.each do |mr|
349:       GraphqlTriggers.merge_request_merge_status_updated(mr.reset)
350:     end
351:   end
352: 
353:   # NOTE: Batch transition merge_status to checking/cannot_be_merged_rechecking.
354:   # This bypasses state machine callbacks for performance but mimics their behavior.
355:   #
356:   # Transitions mirror the `mark_as_checking` event defined above.
357:   def self.batch_mark_as_checking(mr_ids, batch_size: 1000)
358:     return if mr_ids.blank?
359: 
360:     mr_ids.each_slice(batch_size) do |batch_ids|
361:       where(id: batch_ids)
362:         .where(merge_status: :unchecked)
363:         .update_all(merge_status: :checking)
364: 
365:       where(id: batch_ids)
366:         .where(merge_status: :cannot_be_merged_recheck)
367:         .update_all(merge_status: :cannot_be_merged_rechecking)
368:     end
369:   end
370: 
371:   def self.batch_clear_merge_error(mr_ids, batch_size: 1000)
372:     return if mr_ids.blank?
373: 
374:     mr_ids.each_slice(batch_size) do |batch_ids|
375:       where(id: batch_ids)
376:         .where.not(merge_error: nil)
377:         .update_all(merge_error: nil)
378:     end
379:   end

381:   # Returns current merge_status except it returns `cannot_be_merged_rechecking` as `checking`
382:   # to avoid exposing unnecessary internal state
383:   def public_merge_status
384:     cannot_be_merged_rechecking? || preparing? ? 'checking' : merge_status
385:   end

762:   def self.order_by_metric_column(metric, direction)
763:     column_expression = MergeRequest::Metrics.arel_table[metric]
764:     column_expression_with_direction = direction == 'ASC' ? column_expression.asc : column_expression.desc
765: 
766:     Gitlab::Pagination::Keyset::Order.build(
767:       [
768:         Gitlab::Pagination::Keyset::ColumnOrderDefinition.new(
769:           attribute_name: "merge_request_metrics_#{metric}",
770:           column_expression: column_expression,
771:           order_expression: column_expression_with_direction.nulls_last,
772:           reversed_order_expression: column_expression_with_direction.reverse.nulls_first,
773:           order_direction: direction,
774:           nullable: :nulls_last,
775:           add_to_projections: true
776:         ),
777:         Gitlab::Pagination::Keyset::ColumnOrderDefinition.new(
778:           attribute_name: 'merge_request_metrics_id',
779:           order_expression: MergeRequest::Metrics.arel_table[:id].desc,
780:           add_to_projections: true
781:         )
782:       ])
783:   end

785:   # Returns the top 100 target branches
786:   #
787:   # The returned value is a Array containing branch names
788:   # sort by updated_at of merge request:
789:   #
790:   #     ['master', 'develop', 'production']
791:   #
792:   # limit - The maximum number of target branch to return.
793:   def self.recent_target_branches(limit: 100)
794:     group(:target_branch)
795:       .select(:target_branch)
796:       .reorder(arel_table[:updated_at].maximum.desc)
797:       .limit(limit)
798:       .pluck(:target_branch)
799:   end

802:     group(:source_branch)
803:       .select(:source_branch)
804:       .reorder(arel_table[:updated_at].maximum.desc)
805:       .limit(limit)
806:       .pluck(:source_branch)
807:   end

813:   def self.sort_by_attribute(method, excluded_labels: [])
814:     case method.to_s
815:     when 'merged_at', 'merged_at_asc' then order_merged_at_asc
816:     when 'closed_at', 'closed_at_asc' then order_closed_at_asc
817:     when 'merged_at_desc' then order_merged_at_desc
818:     when 'closed_at_desc' then order_closed_at_desc
819:     else
820:       super
821:     end
822:   end

879:   # Returns all the merge requests from an ActiveRecord:Relation.
880:   #
881:   # This method uses a UNION as it usually operates on the result of
882:   # ProjectsFinder#execute. PostgreSQL in particular doesn't always like queries
883:   # using multiple sub-queries especially when combined with an OR statement.
884:   # UNIONs on the other hand perform much better in these cases.
885:   #
886:   # relation - An ActiveRecord::Relation that returns a list of Projects.
887:   #
888:   # Returns an ActiveRecord::Relation.
889:   def self.in_projects(relation)
890:     # unscoping unnecessary conditions that'll be applied
891:     # when executing `where("merge_requests.id IN (#{union.to_sql})")`
892:     source = unscoped.where(source_project_id: relation)
893:     target = unscoped.where(target_project_id: relation)
894: 
895:     from_union([source, target])
896:   end
897: 
898:   # This is used after project import, to reset the IDs to the correct
899:   # values. It is not intended to be called without having already scoped the
900:   # relation.
901:   #
902:   # Only set `regular` merge request diffs as latest so `merge_head` diff
903:   # won't be considered as `MergeRequest#merge_request_diff`.
904:   def self.set_latest_merge_request_diff_ids!
905:     update = "
906:       latest_merge_request_diff_id = (
907:         SELECT MAX(id)
908:         FROM merge_request_diffs
909:         WHERE merge_requests.id = merge_request_diffs.merge_request_id
910:         AND merge_request_diffs.diff_type = #{MergeRequestDiff.diff_types[:regular]}
911:       )".squish
912: 
913:     self.each_batch do |batch|
914:       batch.update_all(update)
915:     end
916:   end

1075:   # Calls `MergeWorker` to proceed with the merge process and
1076:   # updates `merge_jid` with the MergeWorker#jid.
1077:   # This helps tracking enqueued and ongoing merge jobs.
1078:   def merge_async(user_id, params)
1079:     jid = MergeWorker.with_status.perform_async(id, user_id, params.to_h)
1080:     update_merge_jid(jid)
1081: 
1082:     # merge_ongoing? depends on merge_jid
1083:     # expire etag cache since the attribute is changed without triggering callbacks
1084:     expire_etag_cache
1085:   end
1086: 
1087:   # Set off a rebase asynchronously, atomically updating the `rebase_jid` of
1088:   # the MR so that the status of the operation can be tracked.
1089:   def rebase_async(user_id, skip_ci: false)
1090:     with_rebase_lock do
1091:       raise ActiveRecord::StaleObjectError if !open? || rebase_in_progress?
1092: 
1093:       # Although there is a race between setting rebase_jid here and clearing it
1094:       # in the RebaseWorker, it can't do any harm since we check both that the
1095:       # attribute is set *and* that the sidekiq job is still running. So a JID
1096:       # for a completed RebaseWorker is equivalent to a nil JID.
1097:       jid = Sidekiq::Worker.skipping_transaction_check do
1098:         RebaseWorker.with_status.perform_async(id, user_id, skip_ci)
1099:       end
1100: 
1101:       update_column(:rebase_jid, jid)
1102:     end
1103: 
1104:     # rebase_in_progress? depends on rebase_jid
1105:     # expire etag cache since the attribute is changed without triggering callbacks
1106:     expire_etag_cache
1107:   end

1365:   def validate_branches
1366:     return unless target_project && source_project
1367: 
1368:     if target_project == source_project && target_branch == source_branch
1369:       errors.add :branch_conflict, "You can't use same project/branch for source and target"
1370:       return
1371:     end
1372: 
1373:     [:source_branch, :target_branch].each { |attr| validate_branch_name(attr) }
1374: 
1375:     if opened?
1376:       conflicting_mr = existing_mrs_targeting_same_branch.first
1377: 
1378:       if conflicting_mr
1379:         errors.add(
1380:           :validate_branches,
1381:           conflicting_mr_message(conflicting_mr)
1382:         )
1383:       end
1384:     end
1385:   end
1386: 
1387:   def conflicting_mr_message(conflicting_mr)
1388:     format(
1389:       _("Another open merge request already exists for this source branch: %{conflicting_mr_reference}"),
1390:       conflicting_mr_reference: conflicting_mr.to_reference
1391:     )
1392:   end

1394:   def validate_branch_name(attr)
1395:     return unless will_save_change_to_attribute?(attr)
1396: 
1397:     branch = read_attribute(attr)
1398: 
1399:     return unless branch
1400: 
1401:     errors.add(attr) unless Gitlab::GitRefValidator.validate_merge_request_branch(branch)
1402:   end

1405:     return unless source_project && target_project

1409:     end
1410: 
1411:     if target_branch.present? && !target_branch_exists?
1412:       errors.add(:target_branch, _('does not exist'))
1413:     end
1414:   end

1437:   def merge_ongoing?
1438:     # While the MergeRequest is locked, it should present itself as 'merge ongoing'.
1439:     # The unlocking process is handled by StuckMergeJobsWorker scheduled in Cron.

1441:

53:   state_machine :state, initial: :empty do

56:     end

58:     state :collected

62:     # Deprecated states: these are no longer used but these values may still occur

80:     if Feature.enabled?(:commit_sha_scope_logger, type: :ops) # rubocop:disable Gitlab/FeatureFlagWithoutActor  -- TODO: No actor needed
81:       Gitlab::AppLogger.info(
82:         event: 'merge_request_diff_by_commit_sha_call',
83:         message: "MergeRequestDiff.by_commit_sha called via #{caller_locations.reject { |line| line.path.include?('/gems/') }.first}"
84:       )
85:     end
86: 
87:     # Need to serialize the SHAs when querying `merge_request_commits_metadata`
88:     # table as `sha` column is binary and AR is not automatically serializing the SHA
89:     # in this case.
90:     #
91:     # There are callers of this scope wherein `sha` is an array of `sha`
92:     # or multiple target project IDs are passed, so we need to handle that as well.
93: 
94:     sha_array = Array.wrap(sha)
95:     serialized_shas = sha_array.map { |s| Gitlab::Database::ShaAttribute.new.serialize(s) }
96:     project_ids_list = Array.wrap(target_project_id)

98:     metadata_query = MergeRequestDiff.joins(:merge_request_diff_commits)
99:                                      .joins(
100:                                        MergeRequestDiff.sanitize_sql_array([
101:                                          "INNER JOIN merge_request_commits_metadata ON merge_request_commits_metadata.id = merge_request_diff_commits.merge_request_commits_metadata_id AND merge_request_commits_metadata.project_id IN (?)",
102:                                          project_ids_list
103:                                        ])
104:                                      ).where(merge_request_commits_metadata: { sha: serialized_shas })
105: 
106:     diff_commits_query =
107:       MergeRequestDiff
108:         .joins(:merge_request_diff_commits)
109:         .where(merge_request_diff_commits: { sha: sha })
110: 
111:     if project_ids_list.any? { |id| MergeRequestDiffCommit.read_new_commits_table?(id) }
112:       diff_commits_query = diff_commits_query.where(merge_request_diff_commits: { project_id: project_ids_list })
113:     end

115:     from_union(metadata_query, diff_commits_query).reorder(nil)

128:   scope :not_latest_diffs, -> do
129:     merge_requests = MergeRequest.arel_table
130:     mr_diffs = arel_table
131: 
132:     join_condition = merge_requests[:id].eq(mr_diffs[:merge_request_id])
133:       .and(mr_diffs[:id].not_eq(merge_requests[:latest_merge_request_diff_id]))
134:       .and(mr_diffs[:diff_type].eq(diff_types[:regular]))

136:     arel_join = mr_diffs.join(merge_requests).on(join_condition)
137:     joins(arel_join.join_sources)

140:   scope :old_merged_diffs, ->(before) do
141:     merge_requests = MergeRequest.arel_table
142:     mr_metrics = MergeRequest::Metrics.arel_table
143:     mr_diffs = arel_table
144: 
145:     mr_join = mr_diffs
146:       .join(merge_requests)
147:       .on(mr_diffs[:merge_request_id].eq(merge_requests[:id]))
148: 
149:     metrics_join_condition = mr_diffs[:merge_request_id]
150:       .eq(mr_metrics[:merge_request_id])
151:       .and(mr_metrics[:merged_at].not_eq(nil))
152: 
153:     metrics_join = mr_diffs.join(mr_metrics).on(metrics_join_condition)
154: 
155:     condition = MergeRequest.arel_table[:state_id].eq(MergeRequest.available_states[:merged])
156:       .and(MergeRequest::Metrics.arel_table[:merged_at].lteq(before))
157:       .and(MergeRequest::Metrics.arel_table[:merged_at].not_eq(nil))
158: 
159:     joins(metrics_join.join_sources, mr_join.join_sources).where(condition)
160:   end

162:   scope :old_closed_diffs, ->(before) do
163:     condition = MergeRequest.arel_table[:state_id].eq(MergeRequest.available_states[:closed])
164:       .and(MergeRequest::Metrics.arel_table[:latest_closed_at].lteq(before))

166:     joins(merge_request: :metrics).where(condition)

169:   # This scope uses LATERAL JOIN to find the most recent MR diff association for the given merge requests.
170:   # To avoid joining the merge_requests table, we build an in memory table using the merge request ids.
171:   # Example:
172:   # SELECT ...
173:   # FROM (VALUES (MR_ID_1),(MR_ID_2)) merge_requests (id)
174:   # INNER JOIN LATERAL (...)
175:   scope :latest_diff_for_merge_requests, ->(merge_requests) do
176:     mrs = Array(merge_requests)
177:     return MergeRequestDiff.none if mrs.empty?
178: 
179:     merge_request_table = MergeRequest.arel_table
180:     merge_request_diff_table = MergeRequestDiff.arel_table
181: 
182:     join_query = MergeRequestDiff
183:       .where(merge_request_table[:id].eq(merge_request_diff_table[:merge_request_id]))
184:       .order(created_at: :desc)
185:       .limit(1)
186: 
187:     mr_id_list = mrs.map { |mr| "(#{Integer(mr.id)})" }.join(",")
188: 
189:     MergeRequestDiff
190:       .from("(VALUES #{mr_id_list}) merge_requests (id)")
191:       .joins("INNER JOIN LATERAL (#{join_query.to_sql}) #{MergeRequestDiff.table_name} ON TRUE")
192:       .includes(:merge_request_diff_commits)
193:   end

195:   class << self
196:     def ids_for_external_storage_migration(limit:)
197:       return [] unless Gitlab.config.external_diffs.enabled
198: 
199:       case Gitlab.config.external_diffs.when
200:       when 'always'
201:         ids_for_external_storage_migration_strategy_always(limit: limit)
202:       when 'outdated'
203:         ids_for_external_storage_migration_strategy_outdated(limit: limit)
204:       else
205:         []
206:       end
207:     end

209:     def ids_for_external_storage_migration_strategy_always(limit:)
210:       files_in_database.limit(limit).pluck(:id)
211:     end
212: 
213:     def ids_for_external_storage_migration_strategy_outdated(limit:)
214:       # Outdated is too complex to be a single SQL query, so split into three
215:       before = EXTERNAL_DIFF_CUTOFF.ago
216: 
217:       ids = files_in_database
218:         .old_merged_diffs(before)
219:         .limit(limit)
220:         .pluck(:id)
221: 
222:       return ids if ids.size >= limit
223: 
224:       ids += files_in_database
225:         .old_closed_diffs(before)
226:         .limit(limit - ids.size)
227:         .pluck(:id)
228: 
229:       return ids if ids.size >= limit
230: 
231:       ids + files_in_database
232:         .not_latest_diffs
233:         .limit(limit - ids.size)
234:         .pluck(:id)
235:     end
236:   end

427:   # Returns commit SHAs for this diff.
428:   #
429:   # @param limit [Integer, nil] maximum number of SHAs to return
430:   # @param mode [Symbol] controls the data source:
431:   #   :auto (default) - uses the preloaded association when available (no metadata preloading);
432:   #     falls back to the metadata DB query when the association is not loaded.
433:   #   :preload - like :auto but also preloads commit metadata for efficient SHA lookup.
434:   #   :force_metadata - always queries the DB via the metadata join, bypassing any preloaded association.
435:   def commit_shas(limit: nil, mode: :auto)
436:     if mode == :force_metadata || !association(:merge_request_diff_commits).loaded?
437:       return commit_shas_from_metadata(limit)
438:     end
439: 
440:     sorted_diff_commits = merge_request_diff_commits.sort_by { |diff_commit| [diff_commit.id, diff_commit.relative_order] }
441:     sorted_diff_commits = sorted_diff_commits.take(limit) if limit
442: 
443:     if mode == :preload
444:       # ActiveRecord::Associations::Preloader works with both arrays and relations
445:       preload_metadata_for_commits(sorted_diff_commits)
446:     end
447: 
448:     sorted_diff_commits.map(&:sha)
449:   end

451:   def includes_any_commits?(shas)
452:     return false if shas.blank?
453: 
454:     # when the number of shas is huge (1000+) we don't want
455:     # to pass them all as an SQL param, let's pass them in batches
456:     shas.each_slice(BATCH_SIZE).any? do |batched_shas|
457:       break true if metadata_sha_exists?(batched_shas)
458: 
459:       merge_request_diff_commits.where(sha: batched_shas).exists?
460:     end
461:   end

486:   # MRs created before 8.4 don't store their true diff refs (start and base),
487:   # but we need to get a commit SHA for the "View file @ ..." link by a file,
488:   # so we use an approximation of the diff refs if we can't get the actual one.
489:   #
490:   # These will not be the actual diff refs if the target branch was merged into
491:   # the source branch after the merge request was created, but it is good enough
492:   # for the specific purpose of linking to a commit.
493:   #
494:   # It is not good enough for highlighting diffs, so we can't simply pass
495:   # these as `diff_refs.`
496:   def fallback_diff_refs
497:     real_refs = diff_refs
498:     return real_refs if real_refs
499: 
500:     likely_base_commit_sha = (first_commit&.parent || first_commit)&.sha
501: 
502:     Gitlab::Diff::DiffRefs.new(
503:       base_sha: likely_base_commit_sha,
504:       start_sha: safe_start_commit_sha,
505:       head_sha: head_commit_sha
506:     )

513:   def diffs_for_streaming(diff_options = {})
514:     fetching_repository_diffs(diff_options) do |comparison|
515:       reorder_diff_files!
516: 
517:       collection = Gitlab::Diff::FileCollection::MergeRequestDiffStream.new(
518:         self,
519:         diff_options: diff_options
520:       )
521: 
522:       if comparison
523:         # Delete the offset_index from options since we don't want to offset
524:         # the diffs we will request given that we are already requesting specific
525:         # paths
526:         diff_options.delete(:offset_index)
527:         diff_options[:generated_files] = comparison.generated_files
528:         diff_options[:paths] = collection.diff_paths

542:   def diffs_in_batch(batch_page, batch_size, diff_options:)
543:     fetching_repository_diffs(diff_options) do |comparison|
544:       Gitlab::Metrics.measure(:diffs_reorder) do
545:         reorder_diff_files!
546:       end
547: 
548:       diffs_batch = Gitlab::Metrics.measure(:diffs_collection) do
549:         diffs_in_batch_collection(batch_page, batch_size, diff_options: diff_options)
550:       end
551: 
552:       if comparison
553:         if diff_options[:paths].blank? && !without_files?
554:           # Return the empty MergeRequestDiffBatch for an out of bound batch request
555:           break diffs_batch if diffs_batch.diff_paths.blank?
556: 
557:           diff_options.merge!(
558:             paths: diffs_batch.diff_paths,
559:             pagination_data: diffs_batch.pagination_data
560:           )
561:         end
562: 
563:         diff_options[:generated_files] = comparison.generated_files
564: 
565:         Gitlab::Metrics.measure(:diffs_comparison) do
566:           comparison.diffs(diff_options)
567:         end
568:       else
569:         diffs_batch
570:       end
571:     end
572:   end

574:   def paginated_diffs(page, per_page, diff_options = {})

576:       reorder_diff_files!

578:       collection = Gitlab::Diff::FileCollection::PaginatedMergeRequestDiff.new(
579:         self,
580:         page,
581:         per_page,
582:         diff_options
583:       )
584: 
585:       if comparison
586:         diff_options.merge!(
587:           generated_files: comparison.generated_files,
588:           paths: collection.diff_paths,
589:           page: collection.current_page,
590:           per_page: collection.limit_value,
591:           count: collection.total_count
592:         )
593: 
594:         comparison.diffs(diff_options)
595:       else
596:         collection
597:       end
598:     end
599:   end

685:   # Transactionally migrate the current merge_request_diff_files entries to

690: 
691:     rows = build_merge_request_diff_files(merge_request_diff_files)
692:     rows = build_external_merge_request_diff_files(rows)
693: 
694:     # Perform carrierwave activity before entering the database transaction.
695:     # This is safe as until the `external_diff_store` column is changed, we will
696:     # continue to consult the in-database content.

700:       MergeRequestDiffFile.where(merge_request_diff_id: id).delete_all
701:       ApplicationRecord.legacy_bulk_insert('merge_request_diff_files', rows) # rubocop:disable Gitlab/BulkInsert

708:   # Transactionally migrate the current merge_request_diff_files entries from
709:   # external storage, back to the database. This is the rollback operation for

712:   # If this diff isn't in external storage, the method is a no-op.
713:   #

715:   # manually (e.g. from a Rails console) to recover diffs when an external
716:   # diff object is missing or corrupted on storage, so retain it.
717:   # See https://gitlab.com/gitlab-com/request-for-help/-/work_items/3386#note_2739146033

719:     return unless stored_externally?

727:       update!(stored_externally: false)

784:   def convert_external_diffs_to_database
785:     opening_external_diff do |external_file|
786:       merge_request_diff_files.map do |diff_file|
787:         row = diff_file.attributes.except('diff')
788: 
789:         raise "Diff file lacks external diff offset or size: #{row.inspect}" unless
790:           row['external_diff_offset'] && row['external_diff_size']
791: 
792:         # The diff in the external file is already base64-encoded if necessary,
793:         # matching the 'binary' attribute of the row. Reading it directly allows
794:         # a cycle of decode-encode to be skipped
795:         external_file.seek(row.delete('external_diff_offset'))
796:         row['diff'] = external_file.read(row.delete('external_diff_size'))
797: 
798:         row
799:       end
800:     end
801:   end

812:   def encode_in_base64?(diff_text)
813:     return false if diff_text.nil?
814: 
815:     (diff_text.encoding == Encoding::BINARY && !diff_text.ascii_only?) ||
816:       diff_text.include?("\0")

840:         data = row.delete(:diff)
841:         row[:external_diff_offset] = file.pos

844:         file.write(data)

846:

851:   def build_merge_request_diff_files(diffs)
852:     sort_diffs(diffs).map.with_index do |diff, index|
853:       diff_hash = diff.to_hash.merge(
854:         binary: false,
855:         merge_request_diff_id: self.id,
856:         relative_order: index,
857:         project_id: self.project_id
858:       )
859: 
860:       diff_hash[:new_path] = diff.new_path == diff.old_path ? nil : diff.new_path
861: 
862:       # Compatibility with old diffs created with Psych.
863:       diff_hash.tap do |hash|
864:         diff_text = hash[:diff]
865: 
866:         if encode_in_base64?(diff_text)
867:           hash[:binary] = true
868:           hash[:diff] = [diff_text].pack('m0')
869:         end
870:       end
871:     end

893:   def use_external_diff?

896:     case Gitlab.config.external_diffs.when

898:       true
899:     when 'outdated'

950:     else
951:       commits = diff_commits.with_users.map { |commit| Commit.from_hash(commit.to_hash, project) }
952:     end

958:   def save_diffs
959:     new_attributes = {}
960: 
961:     if compare.commits.empty?
962:       new_attributes[:state] = :empty
963:     else
964:       diff_collection = compare_diffs_preloaded
965:       new_attributes[:real_size] = diff_collection.real_size
966: 
967:       if diff_collection.any?
968:         new_attributes[:state] = :collected
969: 
970:         rows = build_merge_request_diff_files(diff_collection)
971:         create_merge_request_diff_files(rows)
972:         new_attributes[:sorted] = true
973:         self.class.uncached { merge_request_diff_files.reset }
974:       end
975: 
976:       # Set our state to 'overflow' to make the #empty? and #collected?
977:       # methods (generated by StateMachine) return false.
978:       #
979:       # This attribution has to come at the end of the method so 'overflow'
980:       # state does not get overridden by 'collected'.
981:       new_attributes[:state] = :overflow if diff_collection.overflow?
982:     end
983: 
984:     assign_attributes(new_attributes)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class MergeRequestContextCommit < ApplicationRecord
4:   include CachedCommit
5:   include ShaAttribute
6: 
7:   belongs_to :merge_request
8:   has_many :diff_files, class_name: 'MergeRequestContextCommitDiffFile'
9: 
10:   sha_attribute :sha
11: 
12:   validates :sha, presence: true
13:   validates :sha, uniqueness: { message: 'has already been added' }
14: 
15:   attribute :trailers, ::Gitlab::Database::Type::IndifferentJsonb.new
16:   validates :trailers, json_schema: { filename: 'git_trailers' }
17: 
18:   validates :merge_request_id, presence: true
19: 
20:   # Sort by committed date in descending order to ensure latest commits comes on the top
21:   scope :order_by_committed_date_desc, -> { order('committed_date DESC') }
22: 
23:   # delete all MergeRequestContextCommit & MergeRequestContextCommitDiffFile for given merge_request & commit SHAs
24:   def self.delete_bulk(merge_request, commits)
25:     commit_ids = commits.map(&:sha)
26:     merge_request.merge_request_context_commits.where(sha: commit_ids).delete_all
27:   end
28: 
29:   # create MergeRequestContextCommit by given commit sha and it's diff file record
30:   def self.bulk_insert(rows, **args)
31:     # Remove the new extended_trailers attribute as this shouldn't be
32:     # inserted into the database. This will be removed once the old
33:     # format of the trailers attribute is deprecated.
34:     rows = rows.map do |row|
35:       row.except(:extended_trailers).to_hash
36:     end
37: 
38:     ApplicationRecord.legacy_bulk_insert('merge_request_context_commits', rows, **args) # rubocop:disable Gitlab/BulkInsert
39:   end
40: 
41:   def to_commit
42:     # Here we are storing the commit sha because to_hash removes the sha parameter and we lose
43:     # the reference, this happens because we are storing the ID in db and the Commit class replaces
44:     # id with sha and removes it, so in our case it will be some incremented integer which is not
45:     # what we want
46:     commit_hash = attributes.except('id').to_hash
47:     commit_hash['id'] = sha
48:     Commit.from_hash(commit_hash, merge_request.target_project)
49:   end
50: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class MergeRequestDiffCommit < ApplicationRecord
4:   include BulkInsertSafe
5:   include ShaAttribute
6:   include CachedCommit
7:   include FromUnion
8: 
9:   CouldNotCreateMetadataError = Class.new(StandardError)
10: 
11:   belongs_to :merge_request_diff
12: 
13:   # This relation is called `commit_author` and not `author`, as the project
14:   # import/export logic treats relations named `author` as instances of the
15:   # `User` class.
16:   #
17:   # NOTE: these columns are _not_ indexed, nor do they use foreign keys.
18:   #
19:   # This is deliberate, as creating these indexes on GitLab.com takes a _very_
20:   # long time. In addition, there's no real need for them either based on how
21:   # this data is used.
22:   #
23:   # For more information, refer to the following:
24:   #
25:   # - https://gitlab.com/gitlab-com/gl-infra/production/-/issues/5038#note_614592881
26:   # - https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/merge_requests/63669
27:   belongs_to :commit_author, class_name: 'MergeRequest::DiffCommitUser'
28:   belongs_to :committer, class_name: 'MergeRequest::DiffCommitUser'
29: 
30:   belongs_to :merge_request_commits_metadata,
31:     ->(diff_commit) { where(project_id: diff_commit.project_id) },
32:     class_name: 'MergeRequest::CommitsMetadata',
33:     inverse_of: :merge_request_diff_commits
34: 
35:   sha_attribute :sha
36: 
37:   attribute :trailers, ::Gitlab::Database::Type::IndifferentJsonb.new
38:   validates :trailers, json_schema: { filename: 'git_trailers' }
39: 
40:   scope :for_merge_request_diff, ->(diff_id, project_id = nil) {
41:     relation = where(merge_request_diff_id: diff_id)
42:     relation = relation.where(project_id: project_id) if project_id
43:     relation
44:   }
45: 
46:   # A list of keys of which their values need to be trimmed before they can be
47:   # inserted into the merge_request_diff_commit_users table.
48:   TRIM_USER_KEYS =
49:     %i[author_name author_email committer_name committer_email].freeze
50: 
51:   # A list of columns we should NOT write to the merge_request_diff_commits table
52:   # when deduplication of commit data is confirmed to be stable.
53:   DEDUPLICATED_COLUMNS =
54:     %i[commit_author_id committer_id authored_date committed_date sha message].freeze
55: 
56:   BULK_INSERT_BATCH_SIZE = 1000
57: 
58:   # Deprecated; use `bulk_insert!` from `BulkInsertSafe` mixin instead.
59:   # cf. https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/issues/207989 for progress
60:   def self.create_bulk(merge_request_diff_id, commits, project, skip_commit_data: false)
61:     organization_id = project.organization_id
62:     partition_enabled = Feature.enabled?(:merge_request_diff_commits_partition, project)
63:     commit_hashes, user_triples = prepare_commits_for_bulk_insert(commits, organization_id)
64:     users = MergeRequest::DiffCommitUser.bulk_find_or_create(user_triples)
65: 
66:     rows = commit_hashes.map.with_index do |commit_hash, index|
67:       raw_sha = commit_hash.delete(:id)
68:       trailers = commit_hash.fetch(:trailers, {})
69: 
70:       author = users[[commit_hash[:author_name], commit_hash[:author_email], organization_id]]
71:       committer = users[[commit_hash[:committer_name], commit_hash[:committer_email], organization_id]]
72: 
73:       # These fields are only used to determine the author/committer IDs, we
74:       # don't store them in the DB.
75:       #
76:       # Trailers are stored in the DB here in order to allow changelog parsing.
77:       # Rather than add an additional column for :extended_trailers, we're instead
78:       # ignoring it for now until we deprecate the :trailers field and replace it with
79:       # the new functionality.
80:       commit_hash = commit_hash
81:         .except(:author_name, :author_email, :committer_name, :committer_email, :extended_trailers)
82: 
83:       commit_hash = commit_hash.merge(
84:         commit_author_id: author.id,
85:         committer_id: committer.id,
86:         merge_request_diff_id: merge_request_diff_id,
87:         relative_order: index,
88:         sha: Gitlab::Database::ShaAttribute.serialize(raw_sha),
89:         authored_date: Gitlab::Database.sanitize_timestamp(commit_hash[:authored_date]),
90:         committed_date: Gitlab::Database.sanitize_timestamp(commit_hash[:committed_date]),
91:         trailers: Gitlab::Json.dump(trailers)
92:       )
93: 
94:       # Need to add `raw_sha` to commit_hash as we will use that when
95:       # inserting the `sha` in `merge_request_commits_metadata` table.
96:       commit_hash[:raw_sha] = raw_sha
97: 
98:       commit_hash[:project_id] = project.id if partition_enabled
99:       commit_hash = commit_hash.merge(message: '') if skip_commit_data
100: 
101:       commit_hash
102:     end
103: 
104:     rows = commit_rows_with_metadata(project.id, merge_request_diff_id, rows)
105: 
106:     transaction do
107:       rows.each_slice(BULK_INSERT_BATCH_SIZE) do |batch|
108:         ApplicationRecord.legacy_bulk_insert(self.table_name, batch) # rubocop:disable Gitlab/BulkInsert
109:       end
110:     end
111:   end
112: 
113:   def self.prepare_commits_for_bulk_insert(commits, organization_id)
114:     user_triples = Set.new
115:     hashes = commits.map do |commit|
116:       hash = commit.to_hash.except(:parent_ids, :referenced_by)
117: 
118:       TRIM_USER_KEYS.each do |key|
119:         hash[key] = MergeRequest::DiffCommitUser.prepare(hash[key])
120:       end
121: 
122:       user_triples << [hash[:author_name], hash[:author_email], organization_id]
123:       user_triples << [hash[:committer_name], hash[:committer_email], organization_id]
124: 
125:       hash
126:     end
127: 
128:     [hashes, user_triples]
129:   end
130: 
131:   def self.oldest_merge_request_id_per_commit(project_id, shas)
132:     # This method is defined here and not on MergeRequest, otherwise the SHA
133:     # values used in the WHERE below won't be encoded correctly.
134:     relation = select(['merge_request_diff_commits.sha AS sha', 'min(merge_requests.id) AS merge_request_id'])
135:       .joins(:merge_request_diff)
136:       .joins(
137:         'INNER JOIN merge_requests ' \
138:           'ON merge_requests.latest_merge_request_diff_id = merge_request_diffs.id'
139:       )
140:       .where(sha: shas)
141:       .where(
142:         merge_requests: {
143:           target_project_id: project_id,
144:           state_id: MergeRequest.available_states[:merged]
145:         }
146:       )
147:       .group(:sha)
148: 
149:     relation = relation.where(project_id: project_id) if read_new_commits_table?(project_id)
150: 
151:     relation
152:   end
153: 
154:   def self.commit_shas_from_metadata(project_id:, limit:, partition_enabled: false)
155:     # Until `merge_request_commits_metadata` records are backfilled, SHAs data may be in found in either table
156:     metadata_join_sql =
157:       if Feature.enabled?(:commit_shas_metadata_lateral_join, Feature.current_request)
158:         # LATERAL with LIMIT 1 fences the subquery against pull-up so the planner does a per-row primary key
159:         # lookup (nested loop) instead of a hash join that scans every metadata row for the project.
160:         <<~SQL.squish
161:           LEFT JOIN LATERAL (
162:             SELECT sha
163:             FROM merge_request_commits_metadata
164:             WHERE merge_request_commits_metadata.id = merge_request_diff_commits.merge_request_commits_metadata_id
165:             AND merge_request_commits_metadata.project_id = ?
166:             LIMIT 1
167:           ) merge_request_commits_metadata ON TRUE
168:         SQL
169:       else
170:         <<~SQL.squish
171:           LEFT JOIN merge_request_commits_metadata
172:           ON merge_request_commits_metadata.id = merge_request_diff_commits.merge_request_commits_metadata_id
173:           AND merge_request_commits_metadata.project_id = ?
174:         SQL
175:       end
176: 
177:     # raw SQL in pluck() bypass ActiveRecord's type casting, so encode() is needed to convert bytea to hex
178:     shas_sql = Arel.sql("encode(COALESCE(merge_request_commits_metadata.sha, merge_request_diff_commits.sha), 'hex')")
179: 
180:     relation = self.joins(self.sanitize_sql_array([metadata_join_sql, project_id]))
181:       .order(:relative_order)
182: 
183:     relation = relation.where(project_id: project_id) if partition_enabled
184: 
185:     relation = relation.limit(limit) if limit
186: 
187:     # rubocop:disable Database/AvoidUsingPluckWithoutLimit -- limit may be applied in the caller
188:     relation.pluck(shas_sql)
189:     # rubocop:enable Database/AvoidUsingPluckWithoutLimit
190:   end
191: 
192:   def self.read_new_commits_table?(project_id)
193:     actor = Project.actor_from_id(project_id)
194: 
195:     Feature.enabled?(:mr_diff_commits_read_new_table, actor) &&
196:       Feature.enabled?(:merge_request_diff_commits_partition, actor)
197:   end
198: 
199:   def self.commit_rows_with_metadata(project_id, merge_request_diff_id, rows)
200:     commits_metadata_mapping = MergeRequest::CommitsMetadata.bulk_find_or_create(
201:       project_id,
202:       rows
203:     )
204: 
205:     rows.each do |row|
206:       row[:merge_request_commits_metadata_id] = commits_metadata_mapping[row[:raw_sha]]
207: 
208:       # At this point, we no longer need the `raw_sha` so we delete it from
209:       # the row that will be inserted into `merge_request_diff_commits` table.
210:       row.delete(:raw_sha)
211:       DEDUPLICATED_COLUMNS.each { |column| row.delete(column) }
212:     end
213: 
214:     rows_without_metadata = rows.select { |row| row[:merge_request_commits_metadata_id].nil? }
215: 
216:     if rows_without_metadata.any?
217:       Gitlab::ErrorTracking.track_exception(
218:         CouldNotCreateMetadataError.new,
219:         message: 'Failed to create metadata',
220:         failed_count: rows_without_metadata.size,
221:         total_count: rows.size,
222:         merge_request_diff_id: merge_request_diff_id,
223:         project_id: project_id,
224:         relative_orders: rows_without_metadata.filter_map { |r| r[:relative_order] }
225:       )
226:     end
227: 
228:     rows
229:   end
230: 
231:   def author_name
232:     commit_author&.name
233:   end
234: 
235:   def author_email
236:     commit_author&.email
237:   end
238: 
239:   def committer_name
240:     committer&.name
241:   end
242: 
243:   def committer_email
244:     committer&.email
245:   end
246: 
247:   def message
248:     fetch_message
249:   end
250: 
251:   def to_hash
252:     super(exclude_keys: [:message]).merge({
253:       'id' => sha,
254:       message: fetch_message
255:     })
256:   end
257: 
258:   def project_id
259:     project.id
260:   end
261: 
262:   def authored_date
263:     has_commit_metadata? ? merge_request_commits_metadata.authored_date : super
264:   end
265: 
266:   def committed_date
267:     has_commit_metadata? ? merge_request_commits_metadata.committed_date : super
268:   end
269: 
270:   def sha
271:     has_commit_metadata? ? merge_request_commits_metadata.sha : super
272:   end
273: 
274:   def commit_author
275:     has_commit_metadata? ? merge_request_commits_metadata.commit_author : super
276:   end
277: 
278:   def committer
279:     has_commit_metadata? ? merge_request_commits_metadata.committer : super
280:   end
281: 
282:   private
283: 
284:   def fetch_message
285:     if ::Feature.enabled?(:disable_message_attribute_on_mr_diff_commits, project)
286:       ""
287:     else
288:       has_commit_metadata? ? merge_request_commits_metadata.message : read_attribute("message")
289:     end
290:   end
291: 
292:   # As of %17.10, we still don't have `project_id` on merge_request_diff_commit
293:   #   records. Until we do, we have to fetch it from merge_request_diff.
294:   # Also, it's possible that `merge_request_diff` is `nil` when accessing this method from an object
295:   # that has not been persisted.
296:   def project
297:     @_project ||= merge_request_diff&.project
298:   end
299: 
300:   def has_commit_metadata?
301:     merge_request_commits_metadata_id.present?
302:   rescue ActiveModel::MissingAttributeError => e
303:     Gitlab::ErrorTracking.track_exception(e, self.attributes)
304: 
305:     false
306:   end
307: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class MergeRequestDiffFile < ApplicationRecord
4:   include BulkInsertSafe
5:   include Gitlab::EncodingHelper
6:   include DiffFile
7: 
8:   belongs_to :merge_request_diff, inverse_of: :merge_request_diff_files
9: 
10:   alias_attribute :index, :relative_order
11: 
12:   before_validation :update_project_id
13:   before_validation :deduplicate_new_path
14: 
15:   scope :by_paths, ->(paths) do
16:     where("new_path in (?) OR old_path in (?)", paths, paths)
17:   end
18: 
19:   def utf8_diff
20:     fetched_diff = merge_request_diff&.stored_externally? ? diff_export : diff
21: 
22:     return '' if fetched_diff.blank?
23: 
24:     encode_utf8(fetched_diff) if fetched_diff.respond_to?(:encoding)
25:   rescue StandardError => e
26:     log_exception('Failed fetching merge request diff', e)
27: 
28:     ''
29:   end
30: 
31:   def diff
32:     content =
33:       if merge_request_diff&.stored_externally?
34:         merge_request_diff.opening_external_diff do |file|
35:           file.seek(external_diff_offset)
36:           force_encode_utf8(file.read(external_diff_size))
37:         end
38:       else
39:         super
40:       end
41: 
42:     return content unless binary?
43: 
44:     # If the data isn't valid base64, return it as-is, since it's almost certain
45:     # to be a valid diff. Parsing it as a diff will fail if it's something else.
46:     #
47:     # https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/issues/240921
48:     begin
49:       content.unpack1('m0')
50:     rescue ArgumentError
51:       content
52:     end
53:   end
54: 
55:   def new_path
56:     super.presence || old_path
57:   end
58: 
59:   private
60: 
61:   def update_project_id
62:     return unless respond_to?(:project_id) && merge_request_diff
63: 
64:     self.project_id = merge_request_diff&.project_id
65:   end
66: 
67:   def deduplicate_new_path
68:     return unless project_id
69: 
70:     self.new_path = nil if new_path == old_path
71:   end
72: 
73:   # This method is meant to be used during Project Export.
74:   # It is identical to the behavior in #diff with the only
75:   # difference of caching externally stored diffs on local disk in
76:   # temp storage location in order to improve diff export performance.
77:   def diff_export
78:     content = merge_request_diff.cached_external_diff do |file|
79:       file.seek(external_diff_offset)
80: 
81:       force_encode_utf8(file.read(external_diff_size))
82:     end
83: 
84:     # See #diff
85:     if binary?
86:       content = begin
87:         content.unpack1('m0')
88:       rescue ArgumentError
89:         content
90:       end
91:     end
92: 
93:     content
94:   rescue StandardError => e
95:     log_exception('Cached external diff export failed', e)
96: 
97:     diff
98:   end
99: 
100:   def log_exception(message, exception)
101:     log_payload = {
102:       message: message,
103:       merge_request_diff_file_id: id,
104:       merge_request_diff_id: merge_request_diff&.id
105:     }
106: 
107:     Gitlab::ExceptionLogFormatter.format!(exception, log_payload)
108:     Gitlab::AppLogger.warn(log_payload)
109:   end
110: end

(End of file - total 110 lines)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class MergeRequestsClosingIssues < ApplicationRecord
4:   include BulkInsertSafe
5: 
6:   enum :link_type, { closes: 0, mentioned: 1, related: 2 }, prefix: true
7: 
8:   belongs_to :merge_request
9:   belongs_to :issue
10:   belongs_to :project
11: 
12:   validates :merge_request_id, uniqueness: { scope: [:issue_id, :link_type] }, presence: true
13:   validates :issue_id, presence: true
14:   validate :from_mr_description_only_for_closes
15: 
16:   scope :with_opened_merge_request, -> { joins(:merge_request).merge(MergeRequest.with_state(:opened)) }
17:   scope :from_mr_description, -> { where(from_mr_description: true) }
18:   scope :with_issues, ->(ids) { where(issue_id: ids) }
19:   scope :with_merge_requests_enabled, -> do
20:     joins(:merge_request)
21:       .joins('INNER JOIN project_features ON merge_requests.target_project_id = project_features.project_id')
22:       .where('project_features.merge_requests_access_level >= :access', access: ProjectFeature::ENABLED)
23:   end
24: 
25:   scope :accessible_by, ->(user) do
26:     joins(:merge_request)
27:       .joins('INNER JOIN project_features ON merge_requests.target_project_id = project_features.project_id')
28:       .where(
29:         'project_features.merge_requests_access_level >= :access OR EXISTS(:authorizations)',
30:         access: ProjectFeature::ENABLED,
31:         authorizations: user.authorizations_for_projects(min_access_level: Gitlab::Access::REPORTER, related_project_column: "merge_requests.target_project_id")
32:       )
33:   end
34: 
35:   class << self
36:     def preload_merge_request_for_authorization
37:       preload(merge_request: [:target_project, :author])
38:     end
39: 
40:     def preload_issue
41:       preload(:issue)
42:     end
43: 
44:     def count_for_collection(ids, current_user)
45:       closing_merge_requests(ids, current_user).group(:issue_id).pluck('issue_id', Arel.sql('COUNT(*) as count'))
46:     end
47: 
48:     def count_for_issue(id, current_user)
49:       closing_merge_requests(id, current_user).count
50:     end
51: 
52:     private
53: 
54:     def closing_merge_requests(ids, current_user)
55:       base = with_issues(ids).link_type_closes
56:       return base if current_user&.admin?
57:       return base.with_merge_requests_enabled if current_user.blank?
58: 
59:       base.accessible_by(current_user)
60:     end
61:   end
62: 
63:   private
64: 
65:   def from_mr_description_only_for_closes
66:     return unless from_mr_description && !link_type_closes?
67: 
68:     errors.add(:from_mr_description, 'can only be true when link_type is closes')
69:   end
70: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true

1830:       .closed_by_message(messages.join("\n"))
1831:       .reject { |issue| issue.is_a?(ExternalIssue) }
1832:       .map(&:id)
1833: 
1834:     transaction do
1835:       update_cached_closing_issues_from_description!(squash_and_merge_commit_issue_ids)
1836:       existing_issue_ids = merge_request_closing_issues.pluck(:issue_id)
1837:       issue_ids_to_create = squash_and_merge_commit_issue_ids - existing_issue_ids

1843:   # If the merge request closes any issues, save this information in the
1844:   # `MergeRequestsClosingIssues` model. This is a performance optimization.
1845:   # Calculating this information for a number of merge requests requires
1846:   # running `ReferenceExtractor` on each of them separately.
1847:   # This optimization does not apply to issues from external sources.
1848:   def cache_merge_request_closes_issues!(current_user = self.author)
1849:     return if closed? || merged?
1850: 
1851:     issues_to_close_ids = closes_issues(current_user).reject { |issue| issue.is_a?(ExternalIssue) }.map(&:id)
1852: 
1853:     transaction do
1854:       merge_request_closing_issues.from_mr_description.delete_all
1855: 
1856:       updated_issue_ids = update_cached_closing_issues_from_description!(issues_to_close_ids)
1857:       issue_ids_to_create = issues_to_close_ids - updated_issue_ids
1858:       next unless issue_ids_to_create.any?
1859: 
1860:       bulk_insert_cached_closing_issues(issue_ids_to_create)
1861:     end

2403:   # Exists only for merged merge requests
2404:   def commit_to_revert
2405:     return unless merged?
2406: 
2407:     # By default, it's equal to a merge commit
2408:     return merge_commit if merge_commit
2409: 
2410:     # But in case of fast-forward merge merge commits are not created
2411:     # To solve that we can use `squash_commit` if the merge request was squashed
2412:     return squash_commit if squash_commit
2413: 
2414:     # Edge case: one commit in the merge request without merge or squash commit
2415:     return project.commit(diff_head_sha) if commits_count == 1
2416: 
2417:     nil
2418:   end

2424:   def can_be_reverted?(current_user)
2425:     return false unless merged_at
2426: 
2427:     commit = commit_to_revert
2428:     return false unless commit
2429: 
2430:     # It is not guaranteed that Note#created_at will be strictly later than
2431:     # MergeRequestMetric#merged_at. Nanoseconds on MySQL may break this
2432:     # comparison, as will a HA environment if clocks are not *precisely*
2433:     # synchronized. Add a minute's leeway to compensate for both possibilities
2434:     cutoff = merged_at - 1.minute
2435: 
2436:     notes_association = notes_with_associations.where('created_at >= ?', cutoff)
2437: 
2438:     !commit.has_been_reverted?(current_user, notes_association)
2439:   end

2441:   def merged_at
2442:     strong_memoize(:merged_at) do
2443:       next unless merged?
2444: 
2445:       metrics&.merged_at ||
2446:         merge_event&.created_at ||
2447:         resource_state_events.find_by(state: :merged)&.created_at ||
2448:         notes.system.reorder(nil).find_by(note: 'merged')&.created_at
2449:     end
2450:   end

2460:   # rubocop: disable CodeReuse/ServiceClass
2461:   def update_diff_discussion_positions(old_diff_refs:, new_diff_refs:, current_user: nil)
2462:     return unless has_complete_diff_refs?
2463:     return if new_diff_refs == old_diff_refs
2464: 
2465:     active_diff_discussions = self.notes.new_diff_notes.discussions.select do |discussion|
2466:       discussion.active?(old_diff_refs)
2467:     end
2468:     return if active_diff_discussions.empty?
2469: 
2470:     paths = active_diff_discussions.flat_map { |n| n.diff_file.paths }.uniq
2471: 
2472:     active_discussions_resolved = active_diff_discussions.all?(&:resolved?)
2473: 
2474:     service = Discussions::UpdateDiffPositionService.new(
2475:       self.project,
2476:       current_user,
2477:       old_diff_refs: old_diff_refs,
2478:       new_diff_refs: new_diff_refs,
2479:       paths: paths
2480:     )
2481: 
2482:     active_diff_discussions.each do |discussion|
2483:       service.execute(discussion)
2484:       discussion.clear_memoized_values
2485:     end
2486: 
2487:     # If they were all already resolved, this method will have already been called.
2488:     # If they all don't get resolved, we don't need to call the method
2489:     # If they go from unresolved -> resolved, then we call the method
2490:     if !active_discussions_resolved &&
2491:         active_diff_discussions.all?(&:resolved?) &&
2492:         project.resolve_outdated_diff_discussions?
2493:       MergeRequests::ResolvedDiscussionNotificationService
2494:         .new(project: project, current_user: current_user)
2495:         .execute(self)
2496:     end
2497:   end

2591:   def collaborative_push_possible?
2592:     source_project.present? && for_fork? &&

2841:   def commit_exists?(sha)
2842:     # We query the SHA from `merge_request_commits_metadata` table first and
2843:     # fallback to querying them from `merge_request_diff_commits` if doesn't match
2844:     # anything. That is to check if commit exists but the records are old and there
2845:     # are no `merge_request_commits_metadata_id` set for them since they're not
2846:     # backfilled yet.
2847:     return true if MergeRequest::CommitsMetadata
2848:       .where(project: project, sha: sha)
2849:       .where_exists(
2850:         MergeRequestDiffCommit
2851:           .where('merge_request_diff_commits.merge_request_commits_metadata_id = merge_request_commits_metadata.id')
2852:           .where_exists(
2853:             merge_request_diffs.where('merge_request_diffs.id = merge_request_diff_commits.merge_request_diff_id')
2854:           )
2855:       )
2856:       .exists?
2857: 
2858:     all_commits.exists?(sha: sha)

2957:   # Returns true when the in-memory state_id no longer matches what the primary
2958:   # database holds. This can happen because the instance was loaded from a
2959:   # replica that lagged behind primary, or because another process transitioned
2960:   # the MR (e.g. lock_mr, mark_as_merged) after this instance was loaded. The
2961:   # caller uses this to skip reload_diff and avoid writing an empty diff on top
2962:   # of a valid merged diff.
2963:   def state_changed_since_load?
2964:     primary_state_id = ::Gitlab::Database::LoadBalancing::SessionMap
2965:       .current(self.class.load_balancer)
2966:       .use_primary { self.class.where(id: id).pick(:state_id) }
2967: 
2968:     primary_state_id != state_id
2969:   end

2988:   def committer_emails_via_metadata_query
2989:     dc = MergeRequestDiffCommit.arel_table
2990:     m = MergeRequest::CommitsMetadata.arel_table
2991:     u = MergeRequest::DiffCommitUser.arel_table
2992: 
2993:     dc.project(u[:email])
2994:       .join(m).on(
2995:         m[:id].eq(dc[:merge_request_commits_metadata_id])
2996:         .and(m[:project_id].eq(target_project_id))
2997:       )
2998:       .join(u).on(u[:id].eq(m[:committer_id]))
2999:       .where(dc[:merge_request_diff_id].eq(merge_request_diff.id))
3000:       .where(u[:email].not_eq(nil))
3001:   end

3009:       .where(dc[:merge_request_diff_id].eq(merge_request_diff.id))

3066:   def with_merge_data_sync(merge_request_block, merge_data_block)
3067:     if dual_write_to_merge_data_ff_enabled?
3068:       # We want to ensure the new merge_data is initialized with the current
3069:       # attributes before we update it.
3070:       ensure_merge_data
3071:       result = merge_request_block.call
3072: 
3073:       begin
3074:         merge_data_block.call
3075: 
3076:       rescue StandardError => e
3077:         Gitlab::ErrorTracking.track_exception(e,
3078:           message: "Failed to sync MergeData",
3079:           merge_request_id: id
3080:         )
3081:       end
3082: 
3083:       result
3084:     else
3085:       merge_request_block.call
3086:     end
3087:   end
3088: 
3089:   def with_single_transaction_merge_data_sync(merge_request_block, merge_data_block)
3090:     if dual_write_to_merge_data_ff_enabled?
3091:       result = nil
3092:       ensure_merge_data
3093: 
3094:       ApplicationRecord.transaction do
3095:         result = merge_request_block.call
3096:         merge_data_block.call
3097:       end
3098: 
3099:       result
3100:     else
3101:       merge_request_block.call
3102:     end
3103:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class MergeRequest::Metrics < ApplicationRecord
4:   include Ci::Partitionable::AssociationFinder
5: 
6:   belongs_to :merge_request, inverse_of: :metrics
7:   belongs_to :pipeline, class_name: 'Ci::Pipeline', foreign_key: :pipeline_id
8:   belongs_to :latest_closed_by, class_name: 'User'
9:   belongs_to :merged_by, class_name: 'User'
10:   belongs_to :target_project, class_name: 'Project', inverse_of: :merge_requests
11:   partitionable_belongs_to_loader :pipeline
12: 
13:   before_save :ensure_target_project_id
14: 
15:   scope :merged_after, ->(date) { where(arel_table[:merged_at].gteq(date)) }
16:   scope :merged_before, ->(date) { where(arel_table[:merged_at].lteq(date.is_a?(Time) ? date.end_of_day : date)) }
17:   scope :closed_after, ->(date) { where(arel_table[:latest_closed_at].gteq(date)) }
18:   scope :closed_before, ->(date) { where(arel_table[:latest_closed_at].lteq(date)) }
19:   scope :merged_by, ->(user) { where(merged_by_id: user) }
20:   scope :with_valid_time_to_merge, -> { where(arel_table[:merged_at].gt(arel_table[:created_at])) }
21:   scope :by_target_project, ->(project) { where(target_project_id: project) }
22: 
23:   class << self
24:     def time_to_merge_expression
25:       Arel.sql('EXTRACT(epoch FROM SUM(AGE(merge_request_metrics.merged_at, merge_request_metrics.created_at)))')
26:     end
27: 
28:     def record!(mr)
29:       inserted_columns = %i[merge_request_id target_project_id updated_at created_at]
30:       sql = <<~SQL
31:         INSERT INTO #{self.table_name} (#{inserted_columns.join(', ')})
32:         VALUES (#{mr.id}, #{mr.target_project_id}, NOW(), NOW())
33:         ON CONFLICT (merge_request_id)
34:         DO UPDATE SET
35:         target_project_id = EXCLUDED.target_project_id,
36:         updated_at = NOW()
37:         RETURNING id, #{inserted_columns.join(', ')}
38:       SQL
39: 
40:       connection.execute(sql)
41:     end
42:   end
43: 
44:   private
45: 
46:   def ensure_target_project_id
47:     self.target_project_id ||= merge_request.target_project_id
48:   end
49: 
50:   def self.total_time_to_merge
51:     with_valid_time_to_merge
52:       .pick(time_to_merge_expression)
53:   end
54: end
55: 
56: MergeRequest::Metrics.prepend_mod_with('MergeRequest::Metrics')

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class MergeRequest::CommitsMetadata < ApplicationRecord # rubocop:disable Style/ClassAndModuleChildren, Gitlab/BoundedContexts -- Same as the rest of the models under `MergeRequest` namespace
4:   include PartitionedTable
5:   include ShaAttribute
6: 
7:   ignore_column :trailers, remove_with: '18.7', remove_after: '2025-11-20'
8: 
9:   # Need this to be set as calling `id` will return [id, project_id] since
10:   # this table is partitioned by `project_id`.
11:   self.primary_key = :id
12: 
13:   partitioned_by :project_id, strategy: :int_range, partition_size: 2_000_000, sequence_name: 'projects_id_seq'
14: 
15:   belongs_to :project
16: 
17:   # This relation is called `commit_author` and not `author`, as the project
18:   # import/export logic treats relations named `author` as instances of the
19:   # `User` class.
20:   #
21:   # NOTE: these columns are _not_ indexed, nor do they use foreign keys.
22:   #
23:   # This is deliberate, as creating these indexes on GitLab.com takes a _very_
24:   # long time. In addition, there's no real need for them either based on how
25:   # this data is used.
26:   #
27:   # For more information, refer to the following:
28:   #
29:   # - https://gitlab.com/gitlab-com/gl-infra/production/-/issues/5038#note_614592881
30:   # - https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/merge_requests/63669
31:   belongs_to :commit_author, class_name: 'MergeRequest::DiffCommitUser'
32:   belongs_to :committer, class_name: 'MergeRequest::DiffCommitUser'
33: 
34:   has_many :merge_request_diff_commits, inverse_of: :merge_request_commits_metadata
35: 
36:   sha_attribute :sha
37: 
38:   # Creates a new row, or returns an existing one if a row already exists.
39:   def self.find_or_create(metadata = {})
40:     find_or_create_by!(project_id: metadata['project_id'], sha: metadata['sha']) do |record|
41:       record.committer = metadata['committer']
42:       record.commit_author = metadata['commit_author']
43:       record.message = metadata['message']
44:       record.authored_date = metadata['authored_date']
45:       record.committed_date = metadata['committed_date']
46:     end
47:   rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique
48:     retry
49:   end
50: 
51:   # Finds many commits by project_id and array of SHAs in bulk.
52:   # The return value is an array of ID and SHA pairs.
53:   def self.bulk_find(project_id, shas)
54:     rows = []
55: 
56:     shas.each_slice(1_000) do |slice|
57:       # rubocop:disable Database/AvoidUsingPluckWithoutLimit -- Already limited to 1K SHAs
58:       rows.concat(where(project_id: project_id, sha: slice).pluck(:id, :sha))
59:       # rubocop:enable Database/AvoidUsingPluckWithoutLimit
60:     end
61: 
62:     rows
63:   end
64: 
65:   # Finds or creates rows for the given project ID and commit rows.
66:   #
67:   # The `commit_rows` argument must be an array of hashes. Each hash should have the following keys:
68:   #
69:   # - :commit_author_id
70:   # - :committer_id
71:   # - :raw_sha
72:   # - :authored_date
73:   # - :committed_date
74:   # - :message
75:   #
76:   # The return value is a hash that maps ID of each found or created commits metadata
77:   # row to a SHA.
78:   def self.bulk_find_or_create(project_id, commit_rows)
79:     mapping = {}
80:     create = []
81: 
82:     # rubocop:disable Database/AvoidUsingPluckWithoutLimit -- This is an array of hashes
83:     commits_shas = commit_rows.pluck(:raw_sha)
84:     # rubocop:enable Database/AvoidUsingPluckWithoutLimit
85: 
86:     # Find commits that are already existing in `merge_request_commits_metadata`
87:     # table. Store them in `mapping` hash so we can map this with rows in
88:     # `merge_request_diff_commits` table by SHA.
89:     #
90:     # `row.last` is the SHA and `row.first` is the ID.
91:     bulk_find(project_id, commits_shas).each do |row|
92:       mapping[row.last] = row.first
93:     end
94: 
95:     # Check if there are commits that don't exist yet in the mapping. It means
96:     # they're not stored in the DB yet at this point so we need to create them.
97:     commit_rows.each do |commit_row|
98:       next if mapping[commit_row[:raw_sha]]
99: 
100:       create << {
101:         project_id: project_id,
102:         commit_author_id: commit_row[:commit_author_id],
103:         committer_id: commit_row[:committer_id],
104:         sha: commit_row[:raw_sha],
105:         authored_date: commit_row[:authored_date],
106:         committed_date: commit_row[:committed_date],
107:         message: commit_row[:message]
108:       }
109:     end
110: 
111:     return mapping if create.empty?
112: 
113:     sha_attribute = Gitlab::Database::ShaAttribute.new
114: 
115:     create.each_slice(1_000) do |slice|
116:       insert_all(
117:         slice,
118:         returning: %w[id sha],
119:         unique_by: :index_merge_request_commits_metadata_on_project_id_and_sha
120:       ).each do |row|
121:         # Need to deserialize sha since it's stored as binary in the database
122:         # and we need to match by its text value.
123:         mapping[sha_attribute.deserialize(row['sha'])] = row['id']
124:       end
125:     end
126: 
127:     # It's possible for commits to be inserted concurrently,
128:     # resulting in the above insert not returning anything. Here we get any
129:     # remaining commits that were created concurrently.
130:     #
131:     # `row.last` is the SHA and `row.first` is the ID.
132:     bulk_find(project_id, commits_shas.reject { |sha| mapping.key?(sha) }).each do |row|
133:       mapping[row.last] = row.first
134:     end
135: 
136:     mapping
137:   end
138: 
139:   def self.oldest_merge_request_id_per_commit(project_id, shas)
140:     # This method is defined here and not on MergeRequest, otherwise the SHA
141:     # values used in the WHERE below won't be encoded correctly.
142:     return [] if shas.empty?
143: 
144:     metadata_results = oldest_merge_requests_commits_from_metadata(project_id, shas).to_a
145:     found_shas = metadata_results.map(&:sha)
146:     missing_shas = shas - found_shas
147: 
148:     # We query the SHA from `merge_request_commits_metadata` table first and
149:     # fallback to querying them from `merge_request_diff_commits` if there are no matches
150:     # This is needed temporarily while `merge_request_commits_metadata_id` is not fully populated
151:     if missing_shas.any?
152:       diff_commits_results = MergeRequestDiffCommit.oldest_merge_request_id_per_commit(project_id, missing_shas)
153:       metadata_results + diff_commits_results.to_a
154:     else
155:       metadata_results
156:     end
157:   end
158: 
159:   def self.oldest_merge_requests_commits_from_metadata(project_id, shas)
160:     select('sha', 'MIN(merge_requests.id) AS merge_request_id')
161:       .where(project_id: project_id, sha: shas)
162:       .joins(:merge_request_diff_commits)
163:       .joins(
164:         'INNER JOIN merge_request_diffs ON merge_request_diffs.id = merge_request_diff_commits.merge_request_diff_id'
165:       )
166:       .joins('INNER JOIN merge_requests ON merge_requests.latest_merge_request_diff_id = merge_request_diffs.id')
167:       .where(merge_requests: {
168:         target_project_id: project_id,
169:         state_id: MergeRequest.available_states[:merged]
170:       })
171:       .group(:sha)
172:   end
173: end

(End of file - total 173 lines)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class MergeRequest::DiffCommitUser < ApplicationRecord
4:   validates :name, length: { maximum: 512 }
5:   validates :email, length: { maximum: 512 }
6:   validates :name, presence: true, unless: :email
7:   validates :email, presence: true, unless: :name
8: 
9:   # Prepares a value to be inserted into a column in the table
10:   # `merge_request_diff_commit_users`. Values in this table are limited to
11:   # 512 characters.
12:   #
13:   # We treat empty strings as NULL values, as there's no point in (for
14:   # example) storing a row where both the name and Email are an empty
15:   # string. In addition, if we treated them differently we could end up with
16:   # two rows: one where field X is NULL, and one where field X is an empty
17:   # string. This is redundant, so we avoid storing such data.
18:   def self.prepare(value)
19:     value.present? ? value[0..511] : nil
20:   end
21: 
22:   # Creates a new row, or returns an existing one if a row already exists.
23:   def self.find_or_create(name, email, organization_id)
24:     find_or_create_by!(name: name, email: email, organization_id: organization_id)
25:   rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique
26:     retry
27:   end
28: 
29:   # Finds many (name, email, organization_id) triples in bulk.
30:   def self.bulk_find(input)
31:     queries = {}
32:     rows = []
33: 
34:     input.each do |item|
35:       name, email, organization_id = item
36:       conditions = { name: name, email: email, organization_id: organization_id }
37:       queries[conditions.values] = where(conditions).to_sql
38:     end
39: 
40:     # We may end up having to query many users. To ensure we don't hit any
41:     # query size limits, we get a fixed number of users at a time.
42:     queries.values.each_slice(1_000).map do |slice|
43:       rows.concat(from("(#{slice.join("\nUNION ALL\n")}) #{table_name}").to_a)
44:     end
45: 
46:     rows
47:   end
48: 
49:   # Finds or creates rows for the given input.
50:   #
51:   # The input argument must be:
52:   # - Array/Set of triples like [[name, email, organization_id], ...]
53:   #
54:   # This method expects that the names and emails have already been trimmed to
55:   # at most 512 characters.
56:   #
57:   # The return value is a Hash that maps input to instances of this model.
58:   def self.bulk_find_or_create(input)
59:     mapping = {}
60: 
61:     # Find existing records with exact matches
62:     existing_records = bulk_find(input)
63: 
64:     existing_records.each do |row|
65:       # Map all found records with triples
66:       mapping[[row.name, row.email, row.organization_id]] = row
67:     end
68: 
69:     # Create missing records
70:     create_missing_records(input, mapping)
71: 
72:     # Handle concurrent inserts
73:     handle_concurrent_inserts(input, mapping)
74: 
75:     mapping
76:   end
77: 
78:   def self.create_missing_records(input, mapping)
79:     create = []
80: 
81:     # Collect records that need to be created
82:     input.each do |name, email, organization_id|
83:       next if mapping[[name, email, organization_id]]
84: 
85:       create << { name: name, email: email, organization_id: organization_id }
86:     end
87: 
88:     return if create.empty?
89: 
90:     # Bulk insert new records
91:     insert_all(create, returning: %w[id name email organization_id]).each do |row|
92:       # Use triples as keys
93:       mapping[[row['name'], row['email'], row['organization_id']]] =
94:         new(id: row['id'], name: row['name'], email: row['email'], organization_id: row['organization_id'])
95:     end
96:   end
97: 
98:   def self.handle_concurrent_inserts(input, mapping)
99:     # Find any records that were created concurrently
100:     missing_triples = input.reject { |item| mapping.key?(item) }
101:     return if missing_triples.empty?
102: 
103:     bulk_find(missing_triples).each do |row|
104:       mapping[[row.name, row.email, row.organization_id]] = row
105:     end
106:   end
107: 
108:   private_class_method :create_missing_records,
109:     :handle_concurrent_inserts
110: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class MergeRequest::CleanupSchedule < ApplicationRecord
4:   STATUSES = {
5:     unstarted: 0,
6:     running: 1,
7:     completed: 2,
8:     failed: 3
9:   }.freeze
10: 
11:   # NOTE: Limit the number of stuck schedule jobs to retry just in case it becomes too big.
12:   STUCK_RETRY_LIMIT = 5
13: 
14:   belongs_to :merge_request, inverse_of: :cleanup_schedule
15: 
16:   validates :scheduled_at, presence: true
17: 
18:   state_machine :status, initial: :unstarted do
19:     state :unstarted, value: STATUSES[:unstarted]
20:     state :running, value: STATUSES[:running]
21:     state :completed, value: STATUSES[:completed]
22:     state :failed, value: STATUSES[:failed]
23: 
24:     event :run do
25:       transition unstarted: :running
26:     end
27: 
28:     event :retry do
29:       transition running: :unstarted
30:     end
31: 
32:     event :complete do
33:       transition running: :completed
34:     end
35: 
36:     event :mark_as_failed do
37:       transition running: :failed
38:     end
39: 
40:     before_transition to: [:completed] do |cleanup_schedule, _transition|
41:       cleanup_schedule.completed_at = Time.current
42:     end
43: 
44:     before_transition from: :running, to: [:unstarted, :failed] do |cleanup_schedule, _transition|
45:       cleanup_schedule.failed_count += 1
46:     end
47:   end
48: 
49:   scope :scheduled_and_unstarted, -> {
50:     where('completed_at IS NULL AND scheduled_at <= NOW() AND status = ?', STATUSES[:unstarted])
51:       .order('scheduled_at DESC')
52:   }
53: 
54:   # NOTE: It is considered stuck as it is unusual to take more than 6 hours to finish the cleanup task.
55:   scope :stuck, -> {
56:     where('updated_at <= NOW() - interval \'6 hours\' AND status = ?', STATUSES[:running])
57:   }
58: 
59:   def self.start_next
60:     MergeRequest::CleanupSchedule.transaction do
61:       cleanup_schedule = scheduled_and_unstarted.lock('FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED').first
62: 
63:       next if cleanup_schedule.blank?
64: 
65:       cleanup_schedule.run!
66:       cleanup_schedule
67:     end
68:   end
69: 
70:   def self.stuck_retry!
71:     self.stuck.limit(STUCK_RETRY_LIMIT).map(&:retry!)
72:   end
73: end

- API versioning considerations for external consumers
- Frontend compatibility checks for all 100+ JS/Vue components
